medical_history.chief_complaint: Complete loss of motor function including ocular movement and eye blinking
medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Diagnosed with clinically probable ALS in November 2012, rapid progression of motor weakness leading to complete loss of voluntary motor functions by early 2015, mechanical ventilator and transcutaneous endoscopic gastrostomy needed since late 2013
medical_history.past_medical_history: Amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS), mechanical ventilator and transcutaneous endoscopic gastrostomy

physical_examination.general_examination: No voluntary or involuntary eye movements, including blinking and corneal reflexes, with verbal commands or external stimuli such as light, tactile, and auditory stimuli. Pupillary light reflexes and cardiovascular response with stimulation were intact. Normal EEG alpha rhythms observed.

other_tests.findings: An online classification accuracy of 87.5% was achieved when Riemannian geometry-based classification was applied to real-time EEG data recorded while the patient was performing one of two mental-imagery tasks for 5 s.
Mismatch negativity (MMN) was confirmed, indicating normal auditory function. P300 components were observed, indicating preserved cognitive function.
Distinct ERD/ERS patterns were observed during left-hand motor imagery (LMI), tongue motor imagery (TMI), and mental subtraction (MS) tasks.

diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The patient was diagnosed with clinically probable ALS according to the Airlie House diagnostic criteria. The diagnosis was supported by rapidly progressive motor weakness propagating to all motor subsystems, including bulbar and respiratory functions, leading to complete loss of voluntary motor functions including ocular movement and eye blinking.
